title: Verilog asynchronous reset pattern leads to corrupted `$check`, `$print` control signals

features.name: procedural display and assert in async reset always block
features.description: Code should include an always block with asynchronous reset sensitivity list (posedge clk, posedge rst) containing $display and assert statements in the reset-conditional else branch
features.snippet: always @(posedge clk, posedge rst) begin if (rst) q <= 0; else begin q <= d; $display("q <= %x", d); assert(!(d & (d - 1))); end end

features.name: procedural assert in sequential logic
features.description: Code should include an assert statement inside a sequential always block with if-else structure for reset handling
features.snippet: always @(posedge clk, posedge rst) begin if (rst) q <= 0; else begin q <= d; assert(!(d & (d - 1))); end end

features.name: procedural display in sequential logic with async reset
features.description: Code should include a $display system task call inside a sequential always block with asynchronous reset and if-else control flow
features.snippet: always @(posedge clk, posedge rst) begin if (rst) q <= 0; else begin q <= d; $display("q <= %x", d); end end

features.name: dual-edge async reset sensitivity list
features.description: Code should include an always block with two positive-edge triggers in the sensitivity list for asynchronous reset behavior
features.snippet: always @(posedge clk, posedge rst) begin if (rst) q <= 0; else q <= d; end
